name: wait_for_workstream
description: Block until one or all of the named child workstreams reach a terminal state (idle, error, closed; `deleted` is also terminal but unreachable in normal operation because hard-delete cascades the row out of storage). Auto-approved — safe, read-only. Use this INSTEAD OF a busy-poll loop of inspect_workstream when you've spawned children and need to wait for them: the tool call absorbs the wait, so the model sees exactly one call + one result regardless of how long the children take. Returns `{results: {ws_id: {state, tokens, updated}}, elapsed: <seconds>, complete: <bool>, mode: 'any'|'all'}` — `results` is a per-ws_id snapshot dict (NOT a top-level mapping), `complete` is True when the wait condition was satisfied vs. the timeout firing. For `mode='any'`, returns as soon as the first child reaches a REAL terminal state (idle / error / closed / deleted) — a denied id alone never satisfies the condition, so a typo'd or foreign id can't false-positive a fan-out wait. For `mode='all'`, returns once every named child has settled (real terminal OR denied). Cross-tenant guard: only the coordinator's own children (or the coordinator itself) are visible — a foreign or non-existent ws_id is reported with state='denied'. Capped at 32 ws_ids per call (excess is rejected as an error rather than silently truncated) and 600s timeout.

ws_ids.description: Workstream ids to wait on. Accepts 1 or more; capped at 32 per call.

timeout.description: Maximum seconds to block. Default 60. Capped at 600 — for longer waits, call again with the same ws_ids.

mode.description: 'any' (default) returns when the first child reaches a real terminal state (idle / error / closed / deleted); 'all' returns once every named child has settled (real terminal OR denied). A pure-denied list with mode='any' short-circuits to complete=false rather than spinning the timeout.
